front: Work: published in Bach's Clavier-Übung II alongside the Italian Concerto in F major
back: Overture in the French Style (Bach, BWV 831)

front: Work: in B minor; originally composed in C minor, transposed to complete the collection's cycle of tonalities; concludes with an 'Echo' movement emphasizing dynamic contrasts
back: Overture in the French Style (Bach, BWV 831)

front: Musical form: slow introduction with dotted rhythms followed by a fast fugal section; developed by Lully; first appeared in operas like Cambert's Pomone and Lully's Atys
